topic: muay thai sparring — first bruise, proud of it

turns.content: I HAVE A BRUISE AND I'M SO PROUD OF IT
Ha! Okay I need context — what happened and why is this a badge of honor?

so I've been doing muay thai for a few weeks and today was my first actual sparring session!! like real sparring with another person trying to hit me. I got kicked in the thigh and now I have this massive purple bruise and I literally cannot stop looking at it

There's something genuinely satisfying about a first sparring bruise — it's physical proof you actually did the thing, not just drilled it. A thigh kick bruise is basically a muay thai rite of passage.

How was the sparring itself? Were you nervous going in?

TERRIFIED. my heart was pounding before we even touched gloves. but then the round started and my body just did stuff?? like the combos I've been drilling actually came out! I landed a jab-cross that my partner said was fast and I threw a teep that pushed her back and my coach was like nice. wallah I was floating for the rest of the session

The fact that your combos came out under pressure means the training is sticking — that's muscle memory doing its job when your conscious mind goes blank from adrenaline. And a functional teep in your first sparring? That's impressive. A lot of beginners forget about it because punching feels more instinctive.

How many rounds did you do?

three rounds of 3 minutes which doesn't sound like a lot but wallah by the third round I was DYING. my lungs were burning and my arms felt like noodles. I sat in my car for 15 minutes after just vibrating from the adrenaline. then drove home and ate the biggest plate of rice and chicken my body has ever consumed. and now I'm icing my thigh and showing everyone the bruise on facetime lmao. beauty is temporary but glory is forever tbh

"Beauty is temporary but glory is forever" needs to be on a gym wall somewhere. Nine minutes of sparring as a beginner is no joke — combat cardio is brutal because you're using every muscle group while managing adrenaline, which burns energy even faster.

The good news is that sparring cardio improves fast. Within a few weeks the third round won't feel like death. Enjoy that bruise — you earned it!
oh I'm enjoying it trust me!! I already can't wait to spar again next week. yalla I need to go put more ice on this thing. thanks for celebrating with me haha

Anytime! Ice 15-20 minutes on, 15 off — and check your stance next session. A lot of first-time thigh kicks land because beginners stand too square. Blade that lead hip and give them less target. Have fun next week!
